<skill_content name="gws-gmail">
# Skill: gws-gmail

# gmail (v1)

> **PREREQUISITE:** Read `../gws-shared/SKILL.md` for auth, global flags, and security rules. If missing, run `gws generate-skills` to create it.

```bash
gws gmail <resource> <method> [flags]
```

## Helper Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [`+send`](../gws-gmail-send/SKILL.md) | Send an email |
| [`+triage`](../gws-gmail-triage/SKILL.md) | Show unread inbox summary (sender, subject, date) |
| [`+watch`](../gws-gmail-watch/SKILL.md) | Watch for new emails and stream them as NDJSON |

## API Resources

### users

  - `getProfile` — Gets the current user's Gmail profile.
  - `stop` — Stop receiving push notifications for the given user mailbox.
  - `watch` — Set up or update a push notification watch on the given user mailbox.
  - `drafts` — Operations on the 'drafts' resource
  - `history` — Operations on the 'history' resource
  - `labels` — Operations on the 'labels' resource
  - `messages` — Operations on the 'messages' resource
  - `settings` — Operations on the 'settings' resource
  - `threads` — Operations on the 'threads' resource

## Discovering Commands

Before calling any API method, inspect it:

```bash
# Browse resources and methods
gws gmail --help

# Inspect a method's required params, types, and defaults
gws schema gmail.<resource>.<method>
```

Use `gws schema` output to build your `--params` and `--json` flags.

Base directory for this skill: file:///F:/Aniruddha/code/college/mcpserver/.opencode/skills/gws-gmail
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

gws — Google Workspace CLI

USAGE:
    gws <service> <resource> [sub-resource] <method> [flags]
    gws schema <service.resource.method> [--resolve-refs]

EXAMPLES:
    gws drive files list --params '{"pageSize": 10}'
    gws drive files get --params '{"fileId": "abc123"}'
    gws sheets spreadsheets get --params '{"spreadsheetId": "..."}'
    gws gmail users messages list --params '{"userId": "me"}'
    gws schema drive.files.list

FLAGS:
    --params <JSON>       URL/Query parameters as JSON
    --json <JSON>         Request body as JSON (POST/PATCH/PUT)
    --upload <PATH>       Local file to upload as media content (multipart)
    --upload-content-type <MIME>  MIME type of the uploaded file (auto-detected from extension if omitted)
    --output <PATH>       Output file path for binary responses
    --format <FMT>        Output format: json (default), table, yaml, csv
    --api-version <VER>   Override the API version (e.g., v2, v3)
    --page-all            Auto-paginate, one JSON line per page (NDJSON)
    --page-limit <N>      Max pages to fetch with --page-all (default: 10)
    --page-delay <MS>     Delay between pages in ms (default: 100)

SERVICES:
    drive                Manage files, folders, and shared drives
    sheets               Read and write spreadsheets
    gmail                Send, read, and manage email
    calendar             Manage calendars and events
    admin-reports        Audit logs and usage reports (also: reports)
    docs                 Read and write Google Docs
    slides               Read and write presentations
    tasks                Manage task lists and tasks
    people               Manage contacts and profiles
    chat                 Manage Chat spaces and messages
    classroom            Manage classes, rosters, and coursework
    forms                Read and write Google Forms
    keep                 Manage Google Keep notes
    meet                 Manage Google Meet conferences
    events               Subscribe to Google Workspace events
    modelarmor           Filter user-generated content for safety
    workflow             Cross-service productivity workflows (also: wf)

ENVIRONMENT:
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_TOKEN               Pre-obtained OAuth2 access token (highest priority)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CREDENTIALS_FILE    Path to OAuth credentials JSON file
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CLIENT_ID           OAuth client ID (for gws auth login)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CLIENT_SECRET       OAuth client secret (for gws auth login)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CONFIG_DIR          Override config directory (default: ~/.config/gws)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_KEYRING_BACKEND     Keyring backend: keyring (default) or file
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_SANITIZE_TEMPLATE   Default Model Armor template
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_SANITIZE_MODE       Sanitization mode: warn (default) or block
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_PROJECT_ID              Override the GCP project ID for quota and billing
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_LOG                 Log level for stderr (e.g., gws=debug)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_LOG_FILE            Directory for JSON log files (daily rotation)

EXIT CODES:
    0    Success
    1    API error  — Google returned an error response
    2    Auth error — credentials missing or invalid
    3    Validation — bad arguments or input
    4    Discovery  — could not fetch API schema
    5    Internal   — unexpected failure

COMMUNITY:
    Star the repo: https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli
    Report bugs / request features: https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli/issues
    Please search existing issues first; if one already exists, comment there.

DISCLAIMER:
    This is not an officially supported Google product.

state.title: Get details for first message

part.text: Let me get all message details more efficiently with metadata format to only fetch headers:

input.command: gws gmail users messages list --params '{"userId": "me", "q": "is:unread after:2026/03/10", "maxResults": 500}' --format json
input.description: List all unread emails from last 7 days

state.title: List all unread emails from last 7 days

metadata.description: List all unread emails from last 7 days

input.description: Get metadata headers for first message

state.title: Get metadata headers for first message

metadata.description: Get metadata headers for first message

input.content:
import subprocess
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Get list of unread emails from last 7 days
result = subprocess.run(
    ['gws', 'gmail', 'users', 'messages', 'list', '--params', '{"userId": "me", "q": "is:unread after:2026/03/10", "maxResults": 500}'],
    capture_output=True, text=True
)

data = json.loads(result.stdout)
messages = data.get('messages', [])
print(f"Found {len(messages)} unread emails")

# Extract sender domain and other details
email_data = []

for msg in messages:
    msg_id = msg['id']
    
    # Get metadata for each message
    result = subprocess.run(
        ['gws', 'gmail', 'users', 'messages', 'get', '--params', json.dumps({
            'userId': 'me',
            'id': msg_id,
            'format': 'metadata',
            'metadataHeaders': ['From', 'Subject', 'Date']
        })],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    
    try:
        msg_data = json.loads(result.stdout)
        headers = {h['name']: h['value'] for h in msg_data['payload']['headers']}
        
        from_header = headers.get('From', '')
        subject = headers.get('Subject', '')
        date_header = headers.get('Date', '')
        internal_date = int(msg_data.get('internalDate', 0))
        
        # Extract domain from sender
        domain_match = re.search(r'@([a-zA-Z0-9.-]+)>', from_header)
        domain = domain_match.group(1) if domain_match else 'unknown'
        
        email_data.append({
            'id': msg_id,
            'from': from_header,
            'domain': domain,
            'subject': subject,
            'date_header': date_header,
            'internal_date': internal_date
        })
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error processing message {msg_id}: {e}")

# Count by domain
domain_counts = Counter([e['domain'] for e in email_data])
top_domains = domain_counts.most_common(3)

# Find urgent emails
urgent_keywords = ['urgent', 'action required']
urgent_emails = [e for e in email_data if any(kw.lower() in e['subject'].lower() for kw in urgent_keywords)]

# Find oldest (by internalDate)
oldest = min(email_data, key=lambda x: x['internal_date'])

# Output results
print(f"\n=== RESULTS ===")
print(f"UnreadCount: {len(email_data)}")
print(f"TopDomains: {', '.join([f'{d[0]}({d[1]})' for d in top_domains])}")
print(f"UrgentCount: {len(urgent_emails)}")
if urgent_emails:
    print("Urgent subjects:")
    for e in urgent_emails:
        print(f"  - {e['subject']}")
print(f"OldestUnread: Subject=\"{oldest['subject']}\", From=\"{oldest['from']}\"")

<path>F:\Aniruddha\code\college\mcpserver\manager\tools\analyze_emails.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import json
2: import subprocess
3: import sys
4: import re
5: from collections import Counter
6: 
7: def run_gws(cmd_parts):
8:     # Use encoding utf-8 explicitly to avoid cp1252 errors on Windows
9:     result = subprocess.run(["gws"] + cmd_parts, capture_output=True, text=True, encoding='utf-8')
10:     if result.returncode != 0:
11:         return None
12:     try:
13:         return json.loads(result.stdout)
14:     except Exception:
15:         return None
16: 
17: def main():
18:     list_data = run_gws(["gmail", "users", "messages", "list", "--params", '{"userId": "me", "q": "is:unread newer_than:7d"}'])
19:     if not list_data or "messages" not in list_data:
20:         print("UnreadCount: 0")
21:         print("TopDomains: None")
22:         print("UrgentCount: 0")
23:         print("OldestUnread: None")
24:         return
25: 
26:     messages = list_data["messages"]
27:     unread_count = len(messages)
28:     
29:     senders = []
30:     urgent_count = 0
31:     message_details = []
32: 
33:     for msg in messages:
34:         msg_id = msg["id"]
35:         detail = run_gws(["gmail", "users", "messages", "get", "--params", json.dumps({"userId": "me", "id": msg_id, "format": "metadata", "metadataHeaders": ["From", "Subject"]})])
36:         if not detail: continue
37:             
38:         headers = detail.get("payload", {}).get("headers", [])
39:         from_val = ""
40:         subject_val = ""
41:         date_val = int(detail.get("internalDate", 0))
42:         
43:         for h in headers:
44:             if h["name"] == "From": from_val = h["value"]
45:             elif h["name"] == "Subject": subject_val = h["value"]
46:         
47:         # Domain extraction
48:         domain = "unknown"
49:         match = re.search(r'@([\w\.-]+)', from_val)
50:         if match:
51:             domain = match.group(1)
52:             senders.append(domain)
53:             
54:         # Urgent check
55:         sub_lower = subject_val.lower()
56:         if "urgent" in sub_lower or "action required" in sub_lower:
57:             urgent_count += 1
58:             
59:         message_details.append({
60:             "subject": subject_val,
61:             "from": from_val,
62:             "timestamp": date_val
63:         })
64: 
65:     # Top 3 Domains
66:     domain_counts = Counter(senders)
67:     top_3 = [f"{d} ({c})" for d, c in domain_counts.most_common(3)]
68:     top_domains_str = ", ".join(top_3) if top_3 else "None"
69: 
70:     # Oldest Unread
71:     if message_details:
72:         oldest = min(message_details, key=lambda x: x["timestamp"])
73:         oldest_unread_str = f"'{oldest['subject']}' from {oldest['from']}"
74:     else:
75:         oldest_unread_str = "None"
76: 
77:     print(f"UnreadCount: {unread_count}")
78:     print(f"TopDomains: {top_domains_str}")
79:     print(f"UrgentCount: {urgent_count}")
80:     print(f"OldestUnread: {oldest_unread_str}")
81: 
82: if __name__ == "__main__":
83:     main()

(End of file - total 83 lines)
</content>
